Evening ride home; warm weather and long daylight make this pleasant, though she avoids the Pride street fair closures by heading west to Belltown.

beats.activity: Quick freshen-up, grab camera and head out to Capitol Hill
beats.reasoning: Pride kickoff is a festive community event; Olga values time with friends and photography, making this a natural Friday evening activity—she's heading to the street fair to document and socialize.

beats.location_name: Capitol Hill Pride Street Fair (Capitol Hill, Seattle)

beats.activity: Organize film photos from tonight, water plants, light reading
beats.reasoning: Wind-down routine: she processes the evening's photography, tends to her plants (they'll need water after a warm day), and settles into evening calm before sleep.
7-hour sleep; Olga is an early riser but also had an active, social evening, so she gets solid rest before her Saturday guide shifts.

diary:
Started the day doing the usual—coffee, plants, bike ride through Belltown to the shop. Georgetown was steady; nothing too wild, just the regular mix of tune-ups and people asking if their chain was supposed to sound like that. Grabbed lunch with some of the regulars at the cafe next door, which was nice. Afternoon flew by between repairs and sketching out routes for this weekend's guided tours.

Left early though, couldn't resist heading up to Capitol Hill for Pride. The energy was incredible—street fair was packed, colors everywhere, and I ran into Maya and Chris near the Pike intersection. Brought my camera and got some great shots of the crowd, the vendors, people just being unapologetically themselves. Walked home through Belltown as it was winding down, felt lighter than I have in weeks.
thought_process: I knew Pride was kicking off today and honestly, I almost talked myself out of going. Work was solid but not draining, and I could've just gone home. But something about a Friday in June with that kind of weather and community energy—I didn't want to miss it. Grabbed my camera on impulse. Being around that many people celebrating felt like exactly what I needed after months of routine. The timing worked out perfectly; I could slip away early, soak it in for a few hours, and still have my evening to decompress. My mood shifted completely once I hit Capitol Hill. Sometimes you don't realize how much you needed to be around your people until you're actually there.
world_event_prompt: Warm, breezy Friday, high of 77F. Seattle Pride kickoff: Capitol Hill street fair and road closures along Broadway and Pike from 5pm. Festive, busy evening downtown.
